info.title: Aithon Shield API (subset)
info.description: REST API for scan workflows. Authenticate with session cookie `sessionId` (browser) or `Authorization: Bearer <aithon_…>` / `X-API-Key: <aithon_…>` for agents and MCP. API keys carry scopes: **read** (GET/HEAD), **write** (mutations), **admin** (full access). Create keys in Settings → API keys. Rate limits (skipped when `AITHON_DEMO_MODE` + dev): global per-IP on `/api` (default 120/min), stricter on login/signup (default 15/15 min), per-key hash (default 60/min). Responses may include `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`, `Retry-After` (429). MCP stdio: `npm run mcp` with env `AITHON_API_URL` and `AITHON_API_KEY`. Optional browser CORS for `/api`: set `AGENT_CORS_ORIGINS` to a comma-separated allowlist.

servers.description: Same origin as the web app

bearerApiKey.description: API key from Settings (prefix aithon_). Scopes: read (GET/HEAD), write (POST/PATCH/DELETE/…), admin (all).

post.description: Session or API key (needs **write** scope). Body: message, optional findingId, scanType (mvp|web|mobile|none), scanId, extraContext. Persists conversation in shield_advisor_conversations. Alias: POST /api/chat. Demo mode returns a canned reply without calling external LLMs.

get.summary: List API keys (metadata only; session required)
get.description: Does not accept API key auth — use a logged-in browser session.

post.description: Returns `plainKey` once. Session required. Default scopes `read` + `write` if omitted.

scopes.description: At least one scope; duplicates ignored.

get.description: 302 redirect to GitHub authorize. Callback: /api/oauth/github/callback → redirects to /settings. Demo mode: 403.
Exchanges code, stores encrypted token, redirects to /settings?git_connected=github or git_error=…
302 redirect to GitLab authorize (GITLAB_BASE_URL). Demo mode: 403.
Exchanges code, stores encrypted tokens, redirects to /settings?git_connected=gitlab or git_error=…

get.summary: List linked GitHub/GitLab accounts (no secrets; session required)
List remediation jobs for a scan (session required)
get.description: Query: scanType (mvp|mobile|web|pipeline|container|network|linter), scanId.

post.summary: Create remediation job (opens PR/MR asynchronously; session required)
post.description: Requires linked Git account for provider. Demo mode: 403.

repoFullName.description: GitHub: owner/repo. GitLab: path_with_namespace or numeric project id as string

get.description: Append-only security audit trail for the current user. Query: limit (1–200, default 100), offset.

get.summary: Download audit log as CSV (session required)
get.description: Up to 5000 most recent events.

get.summary: List organizations for current user (session required)
get.description: Returns id, name, slug, role (owner | admin | developer | viewer | auditor), createdAt for each membership.

No auth. When AITHON_DEMO_MODE=true and NODE_ENV=development, demoMode is true and UI shows the demo banner. Sign-up is disabled; API key create is allowed for local testing. demoApiKeysDisabled is false. demoStrictScanTargets is true when AITHON_DEMO_STRICT_SCAN_TARGETS is set (stronger dummy-URL hints in the UI only; API does not reject scan targets).

No auth. Returns ok, demoMode, port, nodeEnv, hasDatabaseUrl, pid, uptimeSeconds, startedAt — use to confirm which Node process is serving the API (after git pull, restart dev if pid/uptime look stale vs new routes).

post.description: Body matches insertMvpCodeScan (minus userId). Optional workflowMetadata object stores techStackHint, securityModules[] (SAST, SCA, IaC, Secrets, optional DAST), mvpDeployKind, mvpWebDeployUrl. Default MVP modules include IaC: Terraform, Dockerfile, K8s/Helm YAML, compose.

get.description: After a successful MVP scan, returns CycloneDX 1.5 or SPDX 2.3 JSON. Query: format=cyclonedx | spdx (default cyclonedx). Auth: session or API key.
When the web scan has workflowMetadata.sourceRepositoryUrl, finds the latest MVP code scan for the same normalized repository and pairs findings by normalized CWE (DAST vs SAST).

post.description: Starts an async job: pulls OCI/Distribution v2 manifest from Docker Hub (registry=docker-hub) or a public custom registry URL (registry=custom, registryUrl). Creates findings with category Container Image: layer summary (LOW), mutable tag latest/stable/develop (HIGH), high layer count (MEDIUM), very large combined layer sizes (MEDIUM), or errors/unsupported registry (HIGH/MEDIUM). No Docker daemon; no filesystem layer extraction or CVE matching in this slice.

get.description: Each finding object may include fixConfidence: score 0–100, explainability string, sideEffectRisk low|medium|high — deterministic heuristic, not ML. MVP SCA findings may include scaReachability: import_referenced | no_import_match | not_analyzed — static import/require heuristic, not a call graph. The Attack path graph page uses this list client-side to build a heuristic MITRE-style phase graph (no separate graph API).

get.summary: Get a single finding by id (Shield Advisor / detail views)
get.description: Response includes `fixConfidence` when enriched (same shape as GET /api/findings list items).

Session or API key (read). Query: days=7|30|90 (default 30, clamped 7–90). Returns timeline (UTC buckets), mttrHours by severity (requires findings.resolved_at), regressionCount (duplicate open vs resolved fingerprint), currentHealthScore. Non-archived findings only.

post.description: Body includes scan_config JSON string: { targetScanId (uuid), scanType: mvp|web|mobile }. Server sets next_run_at ~60s out. MVP execution is supported by the in-process engine; web/mobile are skipped until implemented.

patch.summary: Update scheduled scan (next_run_at reset when frequency/cron changes)

post.summary: GitHub Check Run + optional PR comment
post.description: Session or API key (write). Uses the user’s linked GitHub OAuth token. Body: repoFullName (owner/repo), headSha, conclusion (success|failure|neutral|…), optional checkName, title, summary, text, pullRequestNumber (issue comment on PR), commentBody, dryRun. Disabled in demo mode.

get.description: Session or API key (read). Returns githubOAuthConfigured, githubConnected, githubUsername, demoMode.

post.description: Session or API key (write). Body: { yaml: string }. Returns ok + normalized config or YAML/Zod errors.
Session or API key (write). Body: { yaml, counts: { critical, high, medium, low } }. Returns pass, violations, failOn.

get.description: Session or API key (read). Returns { entries }.

post.description: Session or API key (write). Body: { cveId, note? }. CVE ID normalized to CVE-YYYY-NNNN+. 409 if duplicate.

patch.description: Session or API key (write). Body: { note?, enabled? }.

post.description: Session or API key (write). Returns { processed, newAlerts }.
Session or API key (write). Sets users.onboarding_completed_at. Returns updated user.

get.description: Session or API key (read). Uses users.sla_policy_hours and open findings. Returns policyHours, breaches, upcoming, openFindingsConsidered.

patch.summary: Update per-severity SLA targets in hours
patch.description: Session required (not API key). Body: optional critical, high, medium, low (number or null to clear). Merges into sla_policy_hours JSON.

get.description: Session or API key (read). Returns { exceptions } with finding title/severity. Expired active exceptions are reconciled on read.

post.description: Session or API key (write). Body: { findingId, justification, expiresAt? ISO datetime or null }. Sets finding status to accepted-risk. 409 if an active exception already exists.

post.summary: Revoke active exception by finding id
post.description: Session or API key (write). Body: { findingId }. Returns the revoked exception row.

post.summary: Revoke a risk exception by id
post.description: Session or API key (write). Returns { ...exception } with status revoked; finding returns to open.

get.description: Session or API key (read). Returns application/zip: manifest.json, README.txt, findings.json, findings-summary.csv, audit-events.json, sla-summary.json, risk-exceptions.json. Audit: compliance.evidence_package_download.
Session or API key (read). JSON attachment: CycloneDX 1.5 BOM with vulnerabilities[] and analysis.state from findings that mention CVE IDs. Audit: vex.workspace_export.

get.summary: CycloneDX VEX JSON — MVP scan findings
get.description: Session or API key (read). Same VEX shape scoped to findings for scan id (scanType mvp). Audit: vex.mvp_scan_export.
Session cookie only. Returns jira and linear objects with connected flags and non-secret defaults (site URL, email, project key, team id).

put.description: Session only. Body: siteBaseUrl (https://*.atlassian.net), email, apiToken, defaultProjectKey?, defaultIssueTypeName? (default Task). Token encrypted at rest. Audit: tracker.jira_connect.
Session only. Body: apiKey, defaultTeamId? (UUID for issueCreate). Audit: tracker.linear_connect.

post.summary: Create Jira or Linear issue from finding
post.description: Session only (not API key). Body: { provider: jira|linear, projectKey?, teamId? }. Disabled in demo mode. Sets finding trackerIssue* fields. Audit: tracker.issue_created. 409 if already linked.

get.description: Session only. Returns array of endpoints with hasSecret flag (secret never exposed). Includes lastDeliveredAt and lastDeliveryStatus.

post.description: Session only. Body: { name, url (HTTPS), format: json|cef|syslog, secret?, eventFilter? (comma-separated event types), enabled? }. Secret encrypted at rest. Audit: webhook_endpoint.created.

patch.description: Session only. Partial body same as create. Audit: webhook_endpoint.updated.

post.description: Session only. Sends a test.ping event to the endpoint and returns { ok, status }.

get.description: Session only. Returns array of event type strings: scan.completed, finding.created, finding.resolved, sla.breached, risk.accepted, risk.revoked, test.ping.

Session or API key (read). Groups non-archived findings by scan (mvp|mobile|web). Returns { generatedAt, cards[] } with score 0–100, grade A–F, open/resolved/accepted-risk counts, open severities, resolutionRate. Cards sorted lowest score first.
